description_long: This airplane features a blocky, pixelated design with a predominantly blue and white color scheme, reminiscent of a stylized or toy aircraft. The wings are broad and straight, extending laterally with intricate, stepped patterns along the edges, and the tail section is similarly detailed with a straight horizontal stabilizer. The fuselage is rectangular and robust, with a slightly raised cockpit area at the front, marked by a lighter blue and white pattern. The undercarriage is not visibly detailed, suggesting a simplified or stylized landing gear. The surface appears hard and matte, with a consistent texture across all components. The overall form is compact and geometric, with no visible engines or propellers, emphasizing a decorative or conceptual representation rather than a functional aircraft.
description: A stylized, blocky blue and white airplane with broad, stepped wings and a rectangular fuselage, featuring a raised cockpit area and geometric detailing.
description_view_0: Front view shows the wide, stepped wings and the raised cockpit area at the nose, with the symmetrical wing pattern clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the thickness of the fuselage, the stepped wing edges, and the geometric tail structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the straight horizontal stabilizer and the full span of the wings, with the tail's blocky design prominent.
description_view_3: Opposite side view reveals the underside of the wings and fuselage, emphasizing the layered, pixelated construction.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with straight, broad wings featuring stepped, blocky edges and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: No visible engines present on the wings or fuselage.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single horizontal stabilizer and a central vertical fin, all rendered in a blocky, geometric style.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit integrated into the front of the fuselage, with a single-seat configuration implied by the compact design.
